items.title: Iteration, mutation and immutability: reject the misconception
items.prompt: Which statement is source-aligned for Java: Beginner to Intermediate and corrects this misconception? Removing directly from an ArrayList inside an enhanced-for loop is safe whenever the removed element is the current one.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Compare each option with both the language-specific boundary case and the cited operational model.

feedback.correct: Correct. Mutate through the traversal mechanism or after traversal, and distinguish an unmodifiable view from an immutable detached copy.
feedback.incorrect: That choice preserves the misconception. Mutate through the traversal mechanism or after traversal, and distinguish an unmodifiable view from an immutable detached copy.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction and rejects the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify source fidelity and ensure the distractor remains plausible without teaching it as fact.

options.text: Mutate through the traversal mechanism or after traversal, and distinguish an unmodifiable view from an immutable detached copy.
Removing directly from an ArrayList inside an enhanced-for loop is safe whenever the removed element is the current one.

answer.explanation: Mutate through the traversal mechanism or after traversal, and distinguish an unmodifiable view from an immutable detached copy.
